pages.raw_text:
In response to an invitation from
the farmers, presented in person by
R. B. Martin. president of the Spo-
kane County Township and Good
Government club, the Spokane Cen
trail Labor council last night ap-
pointed a committee of five to con-
fer with like committee from the
farmers and the equal suffrage or
ganization in the preparation of a
rally for good government to be
held early in August. Mr.. Martins
remarks in urging the action were
received with approval and the mo-
.tion to unite with the farmers was
carried without opposition.


On the committee from the Cen
trail Labor council the following
were appointed: w. J. Coates, ]. r.
Morford, Frank Gould, John Gordon
and H. A. Jarvis. The joint commit
tees will meet Friday night in the
equal suffrage rooms in the Hutton
building
